observation: bishop_librarian_gate.py extended from Write/Edit/StrReplace to ALSO gate Bash and MCP-tool calls. Two largest bypass paths are now covered. Substrate tools (brief_me, consult_scribes, run_session_start, refresh_substrate_cache) always allowed without cache check — they ARE the consult. Reduction-to-practice for A&A #2310 Claim 2 (PreToolUse hook blocks execution when no consultation timestamp found).

Cache file at ~/.lb-session/substrate_cache.json holds {ts, session_task, briefing, cached_at}. Written by mcp__librarian__brief_me MCP tool on every call. Session window = 8 hours (28800 seconds). Gate checks 0 <= age <= SESSION_WINDOW_SECONDS to prevent future-timestamp false-positives. utf-8-sig encoding used for BOM tolerance (PowerShell Set-Content writes BOM on Windows).

Design choice: file-based cache (not in-memory dict) was chosen because the hook script is a subprocess (not persistent process). Each PreToolUse call spawns a new Python process; in-memory state would be lost between calls. File is the only viable persistent-across-invocations storage for a hook architecture.

Windows VM clock skew: PowerShell ([datetime]::UtcNow - epoch).TotalSeconds returns ~6h ahead of Python time.time(). Root cause: underlying hypervisor clock vs Windows guest clock drift. Production-safe: Node.js Date.now() and Python time.time() both read Windows system clock → same value. Test harness must use Python to write test cache timestamps; PowerShell epoch calculation cannot be used for consistency.

refresh_substrate_cache MCP tool added to librarian-mcp/src/server.ts. Re-runs brief_me logic (calls buildBriefing), writes new cache. task parameter optional: if omitted, reads session_task from existing cache file. Intended use: post-rebuild or when canonical state changes mid-session. PostToolUse hook matcher extended to include refresh_substrate_cache so timestamp file stays in sync.

Single gate script handles all tool classes (Write/Edit, Bash, MCP) via internal tool_name branching. settings.json registers three PreToolUse matchers all pointing to the same python command. This is preferable to three separate scripts: shared constants (SUBSTRATE_CACHE_PATH, SESSION_WINDOW_SECONDS, MCP_SUBSTRATE_TOOLS), single maintenance surface, single fail-safe logic.

Wing engine (K514) for Write/Edit untouched. discipline_wing.engine.evaluate() still called for file-content Augur checks. Cache check is orthogonal: Augur-Librarian inside engine still reads legacy timestamp file ~/.claude/state/bishop_last_librarian_consult.ts for its own gate. New cache file is ADDITIONAL enforcement for Bash/MCP paths; does not replace Wing for Write/Edit.

K520.5 phase close: 8/8 verification checks pass. Files modified: ~/.claude/hooks/bishop_librarian_gate.py (hook extension), librarian-mcp/src/server.ts (writeSubstrateCache function + brief_me injection + refresh_substrate_cache tool), ~/.claude/settings.json (Bash + mcp__.* PreToolUse + PostToolUse refresh). Files created: synapse_K520.5.jsonl, REPORT_KNIGHT_K520.5_B127.md. TypeScript compiles clean (npx tsc --noEmit exit 0).
